name: Parana (Brazil)

colors.symbolism: the forests and natural wealth of Parana
the night sky over Curitiba on 19 December 1853, when Parana was elevated to a province
wheat, recalling the agricultural wealth of the state

description: A green field crossed by a wide white diagonal band running from upper-hoist to lower-fly; centred on the band is a blue celestial sphere bearing the Southern Cross and the inscription ESTADO DO PARANA, flanked by a wheat sprig and a sprig of mate.
history: First adopted in 1892 shortly after the Brazilian Republic; design refined and standardised by State Law No. 2.457 of 9 December 1955, with the modern arms regulated by subsequent state legislation.

features.symbolism: A wide white diagonal band across a green field bears the state's central emblem; the green corners and central sphere echo the national flag's republican symbolism
depicts the night sky over Curitiba at the moment Parana was elevated to a province on 19 December 1853, charged with the Southern Cross constellation
Parana's agricultural production, especially of wheat
the mate tree, a major economic crop of Parana
ESTADO DO PARANA inscribed in white capitals across the central blue sphere
